mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is a Python SDK and Proxy Server that allows you to call over 100 LLM APIs in a unified format. It is ideal for applications requiring AI capabilities with cost tracking and load balancing. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and potential costs associated with API usage.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the necessary command-line tools to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

pip install litellm==1.82.dev2

kubectl version --client

helm version

```

> Install the LiteLLM SDK.
> Verify kubectl installation.
> Verify Helm installation.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local kubeconfig to connect to the new cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Authenticate kubectl with the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Deploy LiteLLM Proxy Server
steps.description:
Deploy the LiteLLM Proxy Server using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add litellm https://berriai.github.io/litellm

helm install litellm litellm/litellm --namespace litellm --create-namespace --version 1.82.dev2

```

> Add the LiteLLM Helm repository.
> Install LiteLLM Proxy Server.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for the LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment litellm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace litellm

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up autoscaling for the LiteLLM deployment.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LiteLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove the LiteLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in the deployment.

```bash

helm uninstall litellm --namespace litellm

gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the LiteLLM Helm release.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM
upgrade.description:
Backup current configurations, upgrade the LiteLLM deployment, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --namespace litellm > backup-litelm.txt

helm upgrade litellm litellm/litellm --namespace litellm --version 1.82.dev2

kubectl get pods --namespace litellm

```

> Backup current deployment state.
> Upgrade LiteLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to LiteLLM Proxy'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LiteLLM Proxy Server may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the deployment status with 'kubectl get pods --namespace litellm' and ensure the service is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the LiteLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize the existing nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized access'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** API keys may not be set correctly in the environment variables.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the OPENAI_API_KEY and other necessary keys are correctly set in your environment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Deployment not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LiteLLM deployment may have been deleted or not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the LiteLLM deployment using the Helm install command.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new LLM APIs and improved logging features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace litellm' showing all pods in the Running state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
